title: STANDARD FOR WHEAT AND DURUM WHEAT CXS 199-1995

sections.section: STANDARD FOR WHEAT AND DURUM WHEAT CXS 199-1995
sections.summary: Codex Standard for Wheat and Durum Wheat (CXS 199-1995), adopted 1995 and amended in 2019.

This Standard applies to wheat and durum wheat grains (Triticum aestivum and Triticum durum) intended for processing for human consumption; it does not apply to club wheat (T. compactum), red durum wheat, durum semolina, or products derived from wheat.
Wheat is defined as grains from varieties of Triticum aestivum L., and durum wheat as grains from varieties of Triticum durum Desf.

Wheat and durum wheat must be safe and suitable for human-food processing and free from abnormal flavours, odours, living insects and mites; maximum moisture is 14.5% m/m (with lower limits for some destinations), ergot (Claviceps purpurea sclerotia) limits are 0.05% m/m for wheat and 0.5% m/m for durum, and extraneous matter limits and definitions apply.
Products must be free from heavy metals in amounts hazardous to health and must comply with Codex maximum pesticide residue limits established for this commodity.
The products shall be free from heavy metals in amounts which may represent a hazard to human health.
Wheat and durum wheat must comply with the maximum pesticide residue limits established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission for these commodities.

It is recommended that wheat and durum wheat be prepared and handled per the Codex General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969) and relevant Codes of Practice, that cleaned product be free from objectionable matter, and that, when tested after cleaning and sorting, the product be free from microorganisms, parasites, and microbial substances (including fungi) in amounts that may represent a hazard to health.

sections.section: 5.1 It is recommended that the product covered by the provisions of this Standard be prepared and handled in accordance  with  the  appropriate  sections  of  the General  Principles  of  Food  Hygiene (CXC  1-1969),  and other  Codes of Practice recommended by the Codex Alimentarius Commission which are relevant to this product.
sections.summary: It is recommended that the product be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the Codex General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969) and other relevant Codes of Practice.

sections.section: 5.2 To the extent possible in good manufacturing practice, the cleaned product shall be free from objectionable matter.
sections.summary: Under good manufacturing practice, the cleaned product should be free from objectionable matter.

sections.section: 5.3 When tested by appropriate methods of sampling and examination, the product, after cleaning and sorting, and before further processing:
sections.summary: Requires the product (after cleaning and sorting and before further processing) to be, when tested by appropriate sampling and examination methods, free from microorganisms, parasites, and microbially‑originating substances (including those from fungi) in amounts that may present a health hazard.

Introduces packaging requirements for wheat and durum wheat, specifying that containers must protect hygienic, nutritional, technological and organoleptic qualities, be made of safe suitable materials that do not impart toxic substances or off‑odours/flavours, and that sacks must be clean, sturdy and securely closed.

sections.section: 6.1 Wheat  and  durum  wheat  shall  be  packaged  in  containers  which  will  safeguard  the  hygienic,  nutritional, technological, and organoleptic qualities of the product.
sections.summary: Obliges that wheat and durum wheat shall be packaged in containers that safeguard the hygienic, nutritional, technological and organoleptic qualities of the product.

sections.section: 6.2 The containers, including packaging material, shall be made of substances which are safe and suitable for their  intended  use.  They  should  not  impart  any  toxic  substance  or  undesirable  odour  or  flavour  to  the product.
sections.summary: Requires that containers and packaging materials be made of substances safe and suitable for their intended use and not impart any toxic substance or undesirable odour or flavour to the product.

sections.section: 6.3 When the product is packaged in sacks, these must be clean, sturdy, and strongly sewn or sealed.
sections.summary: States that when the product is packaged in sacks, the sacks must be clean, sturdy, and strongly sewn or sealed.

Sets specific labelling provisions supplementing the General Standard, including that the product name must be 'wheat' or 'durum wheat' and that non‑retail container labelling must include product name, lot identification and manufacturer/packer details (with limited allowance to use an identification mark linked to accompanying documents).
Requires the product name on the label to be 'wheat' or 'durum wheat' as applicable.

Provides that information for non‑retail containers may be on the container or accompanying documents but mandates that the product name, lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer appear on the container, although lot id and name/address may be replaced by an identification mark if clearly linked to accompanying documents.

Mandates that methods of analysis and sampling used to check compliance with this standard shall be those in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the standard's provisions.
